wrong_choice_explanations.が: が marks subjects — it doesn't fit here because 学校 is the destination, not the subject performing the action.
wrong_choice_explanations.を: を marks direct objects or routes passed through — it doesn't fit here because 学校 is the destination being reached, not an object being acted upon or passed through.
wrong_choice_explanations.で: で indicates location where an action occurs — it doesn't fit here because 学校 is where we're going to, not where the action of going takes place.

wrong_choice_explanations.が: が marks subjects — it doesn't fit here because 三時 is a time specification, not the subject of the sentence.
wrong_choice_explanations.を: を marks direct objects — it doesn't fit here because 三時 is a time when something happens, not an object being acted upon.
wrong_choice_explanations.は: は marks topics — it doesn't fit here because 三時 is a specific time point, not a topic for general discussion.

exercises.example_sentence: 友達__プレゼントをあげました。
exercises.translation: I gave a present to my friend.

wrong_choice_explanations.が: が marks subjects — it doesn't fit here because 友達 is the recipient of the gift, not the subject giving it.
wrong_choice_explanations.を: を marks direct objects — it doesn't fit here because 友達 is the recipient (indirect object), while プレゼント is the direct object.
wrong_choice_explanations.と: と means 'with' or 'and' — it doesn't fit here because 友達 is receiving the gift, not accompanying the giver.

wrong_choice_explanations.が: が marks subjects — it doesn't fit here because 家 is the destination, not the subject performing the action.
wrong_choice_explanations.を: を marks direct objects or departure points with certain verbs — it doesn't fit here because 家 is where we're going back to, not what we're leaving.
wrong_choice_explanations.で: で indicates location of action — it doesn't fit here because 家 is the destination we're returning to, not where the action of returning occurs.

exercises.translation: There is a book on the desk.

wrong_choice_explanations.が: が marks subjects — it doesn't fit here because 机の上 is the location where something exists, not the subject of existence.
wrong_choice_explanations.を: を marks direct objects — it doesn't fit here because 机の上 is a location, not an object being acted upon.
wrong_choice_explanations.は: は marks topics — it doesn't fit here because 机の上 specifies the location of existence, not a topic for discussion.

exercises.example_sentence: 日曜日__買い物をします。

wrong_choice_explanations.が: が marks subjects — it doesn't fit here because 日曜日 specifies when the action occurs, not who performs it.
wrong_choice_explanations.を: を marks direct objects — it doesn't fit here because 日曜日 is a time specification, not an object being acted upon.
wrong_choice_explanations.で: で can indicate location or means — it doesn't fit here because 日曜日 specifies when, not where or how the shopping occurs.

wrong_choice_explanations.が: が marks subjects — it doesn't fit here because 駅 is the destination reached, not the subject performing the arriving.
wrong_choice_explanations.を: を can mark routes or departure points — it doesn't fit here because 駅 is where we arrived, not what we passed through or left.
wrong_choice_explanations.から: から means 'from' — it doesn't fit here because 駅 is where we arrived, not where we came from.

exercises.translation: I asked the teacher a question.

wrong_choice_explanations.が: が marks subjects — it doesn't fit here because 先生 is who received the question, not who asked it.
wrong_choice_explanations.を: を marks direct objects — it doesn't fit here because 先生 is the person being asked (indirect object), not the question itself (direct object).
wrong_choice_explanations.と: と means 'with' — it doesn't fit here because we're directing a question to the teacher, not asking together with the teacher.

wrong_choice_explanations.が: が marks subjects — it doesn't fit here because 部屋 is the destination being entered, not the subject doing the entering.
wrong_choice_explanations.を: を can mark spaces passed through — it doesn't fit here because 部屋 is being entered into, not passed through or exited.
wrong_choice_explanations.で: で indicates location where action occurs — it doesn't fit here because 部屋 is where we're going into, not where the entering action takes place.

wrong_choice_explanations.が: が marks subjects — it doesn't fit here because 母 is who receives the call, not who makes it.
wrong_choice_explanations.を: を marks direct objects — it doesn't fit here because 母 is the recipient of the call (indirect object), while 電話 is the direct object.
wrong_choice_explanations.と: と means 'with' — it doesn't fit here because we're calling to the mother, not calling together with the mother.
